model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Sign In button
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (184,426) said dx=356 dy=59 conf=0.90 -> (540,485)
  iter 1: at (540,485) said dx=78 dy=0 conf=0.90 -> (618,485)
Call report_offset().

The crosshair is to the right of the center.
If width is 1000, x=618 is to the right of center.
The "Sign In" text is centered in the button, and the button is centered in the white box.
So the target x should be 500.
If current x is 618, then dx = 500 - 618 = -118.
Wait, if target is 500 and current is 618, I should move LEFT.
But in the image, the crosshair is to the LEFT of the text "Sign In".
This means the target x must be greater than 618.
This implies the button is not centered at 500, or the coordinate system is different.

Current x is 618.
Target x (center of button) is 640.
dx = 640 - 618 = 22.

The center of "Sign In" is about 30-40 pixels to the right of 'S'.
So dx = 50 seems reasonable.

The center of the button is likely where the text "Sign In" is centered.
The distance looks like it's around 40-60 pixels.
I'll guess dx=50, dy=0.
Confidence is high because it's a clear target. 0.9.
